content: read reviews/556.md. if the summary section of the checklist review indicates the review passed, do nothing. if it failed, make relevant updates that are needed to deepmind/556.lean accordingly. if in doubt, reference CHECKLIST.md, which was the source document used when a previous instance of claude produced reviews/556.md.

    16→- [x] No other imports are present.
    17→
    18→## 3. Module Docstring
    19→

    21→- [x] Starts with a heading: `# Erdős Problem 556`.
    22→- [x] Contains a `*Reference:*` line with a markdown link to `erdosproblems.com/556`.
    23→- [x] Includes a brief plain-English description of the problem ("A problem of Bondy and Erdős. This inequality is best possible for odd $n$.").
    24→- [x] Bibliography entries present: `[Er81]` and `[Er81c]`.
    25→
    26→**Note:** The `[Er81c]` entry (line 28) is incomplete — it has author and year but no title: `Erdős, P., (1981).` Consider adding the paper title for completeness.
    27→
    28→## 4. Namespace
    29→
    30→- [x] All declarations are inside `namespace Erdos556 ... end Erdos556`.
    31→- [x] Namespace name uses `UpperCamelCase` (`Erdos556`).
    32→- [x] `open SimpleGraph` appears **before** the namespace block (line 31, before namespace on line 33).
    33→
    34→## 5. Custom Definitions
    35→
    36→- [x] `cycleGraph` (line 37) has a LaTeX docstring (lines 35–36) describing the cycle graph $C_m$.
    37→- [x] `multicolorRamseyNumber` (line 48) has a LaTeX docstring (lines 42–47) describing the $k$-colour Ramsey number.
    38→- [x] `multicolorRamseyNumber` is marked `noncomputable` (uses `sInf`, which involves classical choice).
    39→- [x] `cycleGraph` is not marked `noncomputable` (it is computable — correctly omitted).
    40→- [x] No `sorry` in any definition.
    41→- [x] Functions use `lowerCamelCase`: `cycleGraph`, `multicolorRamseyNumber`.
    42→- N/A: No custom types or Props defined (no `UpperCamelCase` check needed).
    43→- N/A: No `local notation` used (none needed).
    44→- N/A: No `@[category test]`/`@[category API]` sanity checks (optional; "consider" item).
    45→
    46→## 6. Theorem Statements
    47→
    48→- [x] Uses the `theorem` keyword.
    49→- [x] Main theorem is named `erdos_556` (snake_case, matches namespace number).
    50→- [x] Has both `@[category ...]` and `@[AMS ...]` on the **same attribute line**: `@[category research solved, AMS 5]`.
    51→- [x] Main theorem has a docstring (lines 55–59) describing the problem and bound.
    52→- [x] Theorem docstring references source papers: `[Er81][Er81c]`.
    53→
    54→### `answer()` usage
    55→
    56→The original problem asserts a bound: $R_3(C_n) \leq 4n - 3$. This is a direct assertion (an inequality), so **Case 3** applies — no `answer()` wrapper needed.
    57→
    58→- [x] Theorem type is `P` directly (no `answer()` wrapper): `multicolorRamseyNumber (cycleGraph n hn) 3 ≤ 4 * n - 3`.
    59→- [x] Parameters `(n : ℕ) (hn : n ≥ 3)` are theorem parameters, which is correct for Case 3 (the quantifier-after-answer rule only applies when `answer()` is used).
    60→
    61→## 7. Category Attribute
    62→
    63→- [x] Exactly one `@[category ...]` attribute: `category research solved`.
    64→- [x] `research solved` is a valid category value ("Solved but not formally verified").
    65→- [x] Proof body contains `sorry`, consistent with `research solved` (formal proof not yet provided).
    66→
    67→## 8. AMS Attribute
    68→
    69→- [x] Exactly one `@[AMS ...]` attribute: `AMS 5`.
    70→- [x] At least one AMS subject number listed: `5`.
    71→- [x] Numbers in ascending numeric order (only one number; trivially satisfied).
    72→- [x] No duplicate numbers.
    73→- [x] `5` (combinatorics) is a valid AMS MSC2020 code.
    74→
    75→## 9. Proof Bodies
    76→
    77→- [x] Uses `by\n  sorry` format (multi-line, acceptable per checklist).
    78→- [x] No `sorry` in `def`, `abbrev`, or `structure` declarations.
    79→- N/A: Not an open research problem, so no restriction on proof sketches.
    80→- [x] No `native_decide`.
    81→
    82→## 10. Variants & Multi-part Problems
    83→
    84→- N/A: No variants present. The problem is a single inequality.
    85→
    86→## 11. Code Quality
    87→
    88→- [x] 2-space indentation throughout.
    89→- [x] Unicode math symbols used where applicable: `ℕ`, `≥`, `→`, `∧`, `∨`, `¬`, `∀`, `∃`.
    90→- [x] No debug code (`#check`, `#eval`, `#print`, `dbg_trace`).
    91→- [x] No `set_option` that disables linters.
    92→- [x] Lines are reasonably short (all under 100 characters).
    93→- [x] File ends with a single trailing newline.
    94→
    95→## 12. Build Verification
    96→
    97→- [ ] `lake build` not run during this review. No obvious issues; expected `sorry` warning only.

file.content:
# Formal Conjectures PR Checklist — `deepmind/556.lean`

Every item must pass before a PR is approved. Reviewers: reject if any box is unchecked
without an explicit justification.

---

## 1. Copyright Header

- [x] File begins with the **exact** copyright block (no leading blank line).
- [x] Year is exactly `2026`.

## 2. Import Statement

- [x] Exactly one import line, immediately after the copyright block (separated by one blank line): `import FormalConjectures.Util.ProblemImports`.
- [x] No other imports are present.

## 3. Module Docstring

- [x] A `/-! ... -/` module docstring appears after the import, separated by one blank line.
- [x] Starts with a heading: `# Erdős Problem 556`.
- [x] Contains a `*Reference:*` line with a markdown link to `erdosproblems.com/556`.
- [x] Includes a brief plain-English description of the problem ("A problem of Bondy and Erdős. This inequality is best possible for odd $n$.").
- [x] Bibliography entries present: `[Er81]` and `[Er81c]`.

**Note:** The `[Er81c]` entry (line 28) is incomplete — it has author and year but no title: `Erdős, P., (1981).` Consider adding the paper title for completeness.

## 4. Namespace

- [x] All declarations are inside `namespace Erdos556 ... end Erdos556`.
- [x] Namespace name uses `UpperCamelCase` (`Erdos556`).
- [x] `open SimpleGraph` appears **before** the namespace block (line 31, before namespace on line 33).

## 5. Custom Definitions

- [x] `cycleGraph` (line 37) has a LaTeX docstring (lines 35–36) describing the cycle graph $C_m$.
- [x] `multicolorRamseyNumber` (line 48) has a LaTeX docstring (lines 42–47) describing the $k$-colour Ramsey number.
- [x] `multicolorRamseyNumber` is marked `noncomputable` (uses `sInf`, which involves classical choice).
- [x] `cycleGraph` is not marked `noncomputable` (it is computable — correctly omitted).
- [x] No `sorry` in any definition.
- [x] Functions use `lowerCamelCase`: `cycleGraph`, `multicolorRamseyNumber`.
- N/A: No custom types or Props defined (no `UpperCamelCase` check needed).
- N/A: No `local notation` used (none needed).
- N/A: No `@[category test]`/`@[category API]` sanity checks (optional; "consider" item).

## 6. Theorem Statements

- [x] Uses the `theorem` keyword.
- [x] Main theorem is named `erdos_556` (snake_case, matches namespace number).
- [x] Has both `@[category ...]` and `@[AMS ...]` on the **same attribute line**: `@[category research solved, AMS 5]`.
- [x] Main theorem has a docstring (lines 55–59) describing the problem and bound.
- [x] Theorem docstring references source papers: `[Er81][Er81c]`.

### `answer()` usage

The original problem asserts a bound: $R_3(C_n) \leq 4n - 3$. This is a direct assertion (an inequality), so **Case 3** applies — no `answer()` wrapper needed.

- [x] Theorem type is `P` directly (no `answer()` wrapper): `multicolorRamseyNumber (cycleGraph n hn) 3 ≤ 4 * n - 3`.
- [x] Parameters `(n : ℕ) (hn : n ≥ 3)` are theorem parameters, which is correct for Case 3 (the quantifier-after-answer rule only applies when `answer()` is used).

## 7. Category Attribute

- [x] Exactly one `@[category ...]` attribute: `category research solved`.
- [x] `research solved` is a valid category value ("Solved but not formally verified").
- [x] Proof body contains `sorry`, consistent with `research solved` (formal proof not yet provided).

## 8. AMS Attribute

- [x] Exactly one `@[AMS ...]` attribute: `AMS 5`.
- [x] At least one AMS subject number listed: `5`.
- [x] Numbers in ascending numeric order (only one number; trivially satisfied).
- [x] No duplicate numbers.
- [x] `5` (combinatorics) is a valid AMS MSC2020 code.

## 9. Proof Bodies

- [x] Uses `by\n  sorry` format (multi-line, acceptable per checklist).
- [x] No `sorry` in `def`, `abbrev`, or `structure` declarations.
- N/A: Not an open research problem, so no restriction on proof sketches.
- [x] No `native_decide`.

## 10. Variants & Multi-part Problems

- N/A: No variants present. The problem is a single inequality.

## 11. Code Quality

- [x] 2-space indentation throughout.
- [x] Unicode math symbols used where applicable: `ℕ`, `≥`, `→`, `∧`, `∨`, `¬`, `∀`, `∃`.
- [x] No debug code (`#check`, `#eval`, `#print`, `dbg_trace`).
- [x] No `set_option` that disables linters.
- [x] Lines are reasonably short (all under 100 characters).
- [x] File ends with a single trailing newline.

## 12. Build Verification

- [ ] `lake build` not run during this review. No obvious issues; expected `sorry` warning only.
- [ ] Custom linters not run during this review.

---

## Summary

**Result: PASS** (all substantive items pass)

The file is clean, well-structured, and fully conformant with the checklist. One non-blocking note:

1. **Incomplete bibliography entry** (§3): The `[Er81c]` citation on line 28 is missing a paper title (`Erdős, P., (1981).`). This does not violate any checklist rule but should be completed for documentation quality.
2. **Build verification** (§12): Not performed during this review; should be confirmed in CI.

content.text: The review result is **PASS**. No changes needed to `deepmind/556.lean`.
